<> use preg replace to find url patterns and replace them with new ones in php $string = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $pattern = "/]+)\"?>http:\/\/[^\">]+<\/a>/"; $replacement = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $result = preg_replace($pattern, $replacement, $string); echo $result; this method ensures all matching urls are replaced regardless of their position in the string a palindrome is a word that reads the same forwards and backwards like madam or level the process of replacing urls is similar to finding and transforming patterns in text always check for proper escaping and use delimiters to avoid conflicts with other parts of the string
